questions.question_text: What is the primary use of a variable in programming?
questions.answer_key: A variable is a named container used to store data values in a program.

questions.question_text: Python is an object-oriented programming language.
questions.answer_key: Python supports object-oriented programming with classes and inheritance.

questions.question_text: Arrange these programming languages by year of creation: Python, JavaScript, C
Define what an API is in your own words.
questions.answer_key: An API (Application Programming Interface) is a set of protocols and tools that allow different software applications to communicate with each other.

questions.question_text: Which format is used for storing and transmitting structured data on the web?
questions.answer_key: Both XML and JSON are widely used formats for data interchange over the internet.

questions.question_text: TCP is the same as UDP in networking.
questions.answer_key: TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) is connection-oriented and reliable, while UDP (User Datagram Protocol) is connectionless and faster but less reliable.

questions.question_text: Arrange these network layers from bottom to top: Application, Network, Data Link, Physical
questions.answer_key: According to the OSI model, the layers from bottom to top are: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application.

questions.question_text: Explain the difference between HTTP and HTTPS.
questions.answer_key: HTTP is unencrypted and insecure, while HTTPS uses SSL/TLS encryption to secure data transmission over the internet.

questions.question_text: What is the maximum resolution for Full HD video?
questions.answer_key: Full HD (1080p) has a resolution of 1920x1080 pixels.

questions.question_text: PNG supports transparency while JPEG does not.
questions.answer_key: PNG supports transparency through an alpha channel, while JPEG does not support transparency.

questions.question_text: Arrange these image formats by file compression: BMP, PNG, JPEG
Define what a codec is and provide an example.
questions.answer_key: A codec is software that encodes or decodes digital media. Examples include H.264 for video, MP3 for audio, and H.265 for advanced video compression.
RAM stands for Random Access Memory, which is a type of computer memory that can be read and written in any order.

questions.question_text: A solid-state drive (SSD) is faster than a hard disk drive (HDD).
questions.answer_key: SSDs use flash memory and have no moving parts, making them significantly faster than traditional HDDs.

questions.question_text: Arrange these storage devices by speed: SSD, HDD, USB Flash Drive
questions.answer_key: SSD is fastest, followed by USB Flash Drive, and HDD is the slowest due to mechanical movement.

questions.question_text: What is the purpose of a firewall in IT security?
questions.answer_key: A firewall is a network security system that monitors and controls incoming and outgoing network traffic based on predetermined security rules.
